global_objective: Add a CLI entry point to scripts/forensics-analysis-validate.mjs (reusing the existing lib/cli.mjs envelope and scripts/forensics.mjs's wrapper pattern), point spec-forensics/SKILL.md's doc at that CLI instead of the direct function import, and fix the judgment-finding signal-anchoring check so it matches a bullet's full multi-line text instead of only its first line.

tasks.justification: Bounded implementation with clear acceptance criteria: a CLI wrapper reusing scripts/lib/cli.mjs's emitSuccess/emitError and mirroring scripts/forensics.mjs's main()/isMainModule pattern, no new design; sonnet per the most-austere-that-qualifies rule.
tasks.instructions: Add a CLI entry point to scripts/forensics-analysis-validate.mjs, per R1.S1 and R1.S2, without changing the existing exported validateForensicsAnalysis(mdText, forensicsJson) signature or return shape. Mirror scripts/forensics.mjs's main()/isMainModule guard: read SPECDIR from argv, read SPECDIR/forensics-analysis.md and SPECDIR/forensics.json, call validateForensicsAnalysis with their contents, and pass its return value verbatim as data to emitSuccess from scripts/lib/cli.mjs (per R1.S1, success envelope regardless of the validator's own ok field; per R1.S2, a validator-reported failure is data, not a process failure — exit code stays 0). A missing SPECDIR argument, or a required file that is unreadable or not valid JSON, is a genuine process error: call emitError(reason) (per the spec's Technical Requirements 'Restricciones adicionales', inherited lib/cli.mjs behavior, not new logic to design).
tasks.expected_output_schema: scripts/forensics-analysis-validate.mjs keeps exporting validateForensicsAnalysis unchanged and additionally runs, only when it is the main module, a CLI entry point that takes SPECDIR as its sole argument, reads SPECDIR/forensics-analysis.md and SPECDIR/forensics.json, invokes validateForensicsAnalysis, and emits the canonical {ok:true,data:<validator return value>} envelope via emitSuccess (exit 0), or emitError(reason) for a missing/unreadable/invalid input.

test_contract.assertion: Spawning the CLI against a SPECDIR fixture whose forensics-analysis.md and forensics.json reconcile (no violated invariant) prints exactly {"ok":true,"data":{"ok":true,"errors":[]}} to stdout and the process exits with code 0.

Spawning the CLI against a SPECDIR fixture whose forensics-analysis.md violates an invariant prints {"ok":true,"data":{"ok":false,"errors":[...]}} with a non-empty errors array, and the process still exits with code 0.

tasks.justification: Redactar documentación siguiendo un esquema ya existente (la sección 'Validating the written doc' de SKILL.md) más un ajuste literal de una aserción de test — sin diseño ni lógica nueva; sonnet doc_writer.
tasks.instructions: Update the 'Validating the written doc' section of skills/spec-forensics/SKILL.md, per R2.S1, to instruct invoking the CLI entry point delivered by cli-entry-point (node ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/forensics-analysis-validate.mjs SPECDIR) and reading data.ok/data.errors from its stdout envelope, instead of importing validateForensicsAnalysis and calling it as a function. Then update test/exec/spec-forensics-skill-doc.test.mjs's AC4 test so its assertions match the new wording while still verifying the same invariant: the doc must reference the CLI script by filename/invocation and must no longer instruct importing validateForensicsAnalysis as a function call.
tasks.expected_output_schema: Updated skills/spec-forensics/SKILL.md 'Validating the written doc' section referencing the CLI invocation (not the direct function import), plus an updated AC4 assertion in test/exec/spec-forensics-skill-doc.test.mjs that matches the new wording and passes.

tasks.justification: Bounded fix to existing matching logic in validateForensicsAnalysis (extend the text a judgment finding is matched against), clear ACs; sonnet. R3's spec 'Depende de:' line is '—', so no functional dependency; per the spec's Assumptions section the two independent pieces of work (this fix and cli-entry-point) are meant to land as two separate commits, which is already honored by keeping them as distinct tasks rather than merging them into one.
tasks.instructions: Fix the judgment-finding signal-citation check inside validateForensicsAnalysis (scripts/forensics-analysis-validate.mjs) per R3.S1 and R3.S2: it currently matches each known signal name against only a bullet's own first trimmed line, so extend it to match against that bullet's full text — its first line plus any continuation lines belonging to the same list item (more-indented lines following it, up to the next bullet/heading/blank separator). Preserve the existing negative behavior (R3.S2) unchanged: a finding whose full multi-line text cites no known signal name must still produce the 'cites no known signal' error. Do not change validateForensicsAnalysis's exported signature or its {ok, errors} return shape, and do not touch any other validation rule.
tasks.expected_output_schema: validateForensicsAnalysis's judgment-finding signal-citation check matches candidate signal names against each finding's full multi-line text (first line plus its continuation lines), not only its first line; the exported function's signature and {ok, errors} return shape are unchanged.

test_contract.assertion: A judgment finding whose first line cites no signal but whose continuation line (2nd/3rd line, indented, same list item) cites a real signal name from forensics.json produces no 'cites no known signal' error for that finding.
A judgment finding whose first line and every continuation line cite no real signal name still produces a 'cites no known signal' error for that finding.

tasks.justification: AC-E2E's CLI + multi-line-signal walkthrough isn't covered by the union of cli-entry-point's tests (simple fixtures, no multi-line finding) or signal-anchoring-multiline-fix's tests (call validateForensicsAnalysis directly, not via the CLI), so a new integration test must be authored — a code_writer node per the E2E-task-assignment rule, not a verifier.
tasks.instructions: Author an end-to-end integration test for R-E2E.S1 that spawns cli-entry-point's CLI (scripts/forensics-analysis-validate.mjs) against a SPECDIR fixture combining the deliverables of cli-entry-point and signal-anchoring-multiline-fix: a forensics.json with a real signal (e.g. orchestrator_share) and a forensics-analysis.md whose judgment finding cites that signal only on a continuation line (2nd/3rd line) of its bullet, otherwise reconciling with no violated invariant. This exercises the same CLI invocation skill-doc-update pointed SKILL.md at, confirming its documented usage actually holds end-to-end. Assert stdout is exactly {"ok":true,"data":{"ok":true,"errors":[]}} and the process exits with code 0. Satisfy AC-E2E.
tasks.expected_output_schema: A new automated test, co-located with the existing sdd-kit CLI/exec tests, that spawns node scripts/forensics-analysis-validate.mjs against a fixture SPECDIR (multi-line judgment finding citing its signal only in a continuation line) and asserts the exact success envelope {"ok":true,"data":{"ok":true,"errors":[]}} and exit code 0.

test_contract.assertion: Running node forensics-analysis-validate.mjs against a SPECDIR fixture whose forensics-analysis.md cites a real forensics.json signal only on a continuation line of a judgment finding prints {"ok":true,"data":{"ok":true,"errors":[]}} and the process exits with code 0.
